content:
The short answer is no. But your BehaviorSubjects are. Here is exactly what to delete, what to keep, and how to bridge the gap using @angular/core/rxjs-interop.

4 min read
9 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

From the very moment Signals came out, the Angular community has been speculating about the demise of RxJS. With Angular 22, where zone.js gets deprecated and the resource() API becomes the way of making standard HTTP requests, you might be tempted to uninstall rxjs altogether.
You should not.
While RxJS remains the integral part of the Angular world, its scope has become reduced to roughly 80%. You no longer have to resort to this Swiss army knife for solving every problem; you just use it as the scalpel to deal with complicated async event streams.
And if you are porting an enterprise-level app into the Zoneless era, then below you will find the precise guide on what to remove, what to keep, and how to create the bridge.
Phase 1: The Great BehaviorSubject Purge
For years, we’ve been misusing BehaviorSubject to store synchronous state since we only had the reactive way. For that matter, if you have a variable that…
